title: The Ultimate Guide to Server Components in Next.js 14
excerpt: Discover how Server Components work in Next.js 14 and how they can improve performance, SEO, and developer experience.

author.name: Mohammad Yasin
author.bio: I build full-stack applications with React, Next.js, Express, and MongoDB.

content:
What Are Server Components?

Server Components are React components that render on the server instead of the client, reducing the amount of JavaScript sent to the browser.

How Server Components Work

They allow you to fetch data directly inside your components and send only the rendered HTML to the client.


export default async function Page() {
  const posts = await getPosts();
  return ;
    }

Benefits of Server Components

  • Smaller JavaScript bundles
  • Improved performance
  • Better SEO
  • Simpler data fetching
  • Reduced client-side complexity

When Should You Use Server Components?

Server Components are ideal for dashboards, blogs, e-commerce pages, and any page that relies heavily on server-side data fetching.

Server Components don't replace Client Components—they complement them.

Conclusion

Server Components are one of the biggest improvements introduced in modern React and Next.js.
